sunset.title: Boat Dock
sunset.desc: Come check out the SSC arsenal of boats and watercraft. Start the week off right on the water!

sunset.title: Boat Dock
sunset.desc: We're open again! Swing by the Boat Dock for some post-dinner fun!

sunset.title: New Camper Orientation
sunset.desc: First summer at Camp? WELCOME HOME! We're so glad you're here. Meet Camp Director Dave Bunnett by the Flagpole for an introduction to your beautiful home for the week!

sunset.title: Intro Meeting
sunset.desc: Join Dave and Stefaney at the picnic tables at the bottom of the Lodge as they introduce you to your new Camp Family! Meet the wonderful summer Staph and watch as they try to outdo each other for your laughter and applause.

sunset.title: Ski Dock & Activity Sign-Ups
sunset.desc: On the round table outside the front office is the place to sign up for Sunday's activities, including rowing, sailing, Naturalist programming, family portraits, Bread Bake, Boatster and Astrocruises. Sign up for skiing in the downstairs Foyer just after introskits. After tonight, future ski dock sign ups can be done down at the ski dock!

sunset.title: Hiking Meeting
sunset.desc: Want to learn more about what your enthusiastic hiking guides have planned for the week? Meet Heather and Jack at the Tallac Room for details on FOUR pre-planned, guided hikes. Feel like venturing out on your own? Drop by for some suggestions and info on trail conditions!

sunrise.title: Rowing*
sunrise.desc: What's a better way to start your first morning at Camp than on the glassy waters of Fallen Leaf? Meet Braun & Tavi for a morning scull on the rowing dock! Come soak up the beauty of this opportunity - all skill levels welcome! Make sure to sign up for a time in the sign-up book by the office first.

sunrise.title: Sunrise Yoga*
sunrise.desc: The camp is quiet, the light is GOLDEN, and the Surya Namaskar is waiting. Sunrise Yoga is your excuse to drag yourself out of that sleeping bag and greet the day before anyone else does (with Prisha of course!). The sun is rising with or without you — don't make it rise alone!! Meet on the Crawdeck!

sunrise.title: Advanced Ski/Board Clinic*
sunrise.desc: How do you like your butter? How about on the surface of Fallen Leaf? Come cut up some flat, glassy water before the rush and ripples of the day. Come work on your wakeboarding and waterskiing form and technique with Mara & Ben. More time on the water, extra tips from ski dock staphers, and a beautiful morning on the lake? Sign me up!—or rather, come sign up on the ski dock for Advanced Clinic from 8am to 10am. Advanced slalom skiers and advanced wakeboarders are given preference but all are welcome!

sunrise.title: Naturalist Hike*
sunrise.desc: Join Isaiah for the chillest guided hike of the week! Expect 3-5 miles on trails you've hopefully never seen before, with plenty of time to soak in the views. As a geologist, Isaiah gives you the opportunity to gain deeper connections with the land by learning about its past and future. This hike is perfect for folks looking for an easier hiker, wanting to see new things, or simply wanting to get outside and chat.

sunrise.title: Boat Dock
sunrise.desc: Wondering where to find the best view at Camp? The view from the middle of the lake is tough to beat! Come check out our boats and explore a bit - can you spot Mount Tallac?

sunrise.title: Guppies
sunrise.desc: Welcome to the jungle, explorers! At 9:15am, swing into Guppy Lagoon to meet your fearless guides and begin your very first adventure in the Jungle. From splashing in the waterfall and kiddy pool to getting creative at the crafts table, there's no shortage of discovery to be had. If we are lucky, we might just get a chance to meet the yoga fairy and learn her ways, too. Bring sunscreen, a change of clothes, and shoes that can get wet. Get ready to let imaginations run wild!

sunrise.title: Arts & Crafts: Bisqueware*
sunrise.desc: Ever wanted to try your hand at ceramics? Come glaze some mugs, plates, and tiles with Nia at the crafts tables by the volleyball court. Remember to pick up the piece you'd like to glaze from the Fountain before heading over. Sign-up for crafts, space is limited!

sunrise.title: Kids Groups
sunrise.desc:
CRAWDADS: Avast ye! Those who would sail under the Crawdad flag must prove themselves worthy in this week of wild adventures and daring trials. Only the boldest and most rowdy crew of sea-dogs shall earn the right to call themselves true pirates! We drop anchor at 9:30 am at our secret hideout in the Angora Room, where all hands will gather to swear by the Pirate Code!

MERGANSERS: A perfect wedding. A couple in love. But no ring. It is up to our young Mergansers, wearing imaginary fedoras and trenchcoats, to solve this 1920s style mystery. But first, setting healthy norms for the group and making sure all of our detectives know one another's names will precede their investigation. The true enquete will begin when we launch our camp-wide scavenger hunt! Make sure you're ready for an adventure, ready to solve a mystery, and most importantly, ready to have fun! We will meet on the Angora deck in both the morning and afternoon. In the afternoon, please wear an outfit that is good for tennis!

CHICKAREES: The owls have delivered your letters, young witches and wizards — welcome to Chickarees. At 9:30am, report to the Picnic Tables where the Sorting Hat awaits. Houses will be chosen, alliances will be formed, and the Triwizard Tournament will officially be set in motion. Once sorted, champions will visit the volleyball courts to play dragonball, earning points that will echo through the halls all week long.

MARMOTS: ZOOOOOOM!! The Marmots are coming in HOT as we embark on our time-travelling journey! Our adventure begins at 9:30am sharp at the picnic tables from which **ROARRRR!!** we will escape the dinosaurs tromping through this prehistoric land and set our sights on the quick-sand pits (volleyball courts) at which point we will acquaint ourselves with our fellow time-travelling companions in a fun name game. Team cohesiveness is KEY for our following games of tag and campers vs counselors.

sunrise.title: Tennis: Beginners Clinic*
sunrise.desc: USTA 1.5-2.5 recommended. Never held a racket before? Not sure how to whip a Federer forehand? Rarely hitting your serves into that diagonal box that only seems to get smaller? Fear no more. In the Beginner Tennis Clinic, Robert will walk you through all the basics—the continental grip, proper footwork technique, basic technique and strategy. This clinic warmly welcomes all introductory skill levels, from years-rusty to never played before—this is your sign. Welcome to the sport you can play for life! Meet at the Tennis Courts!

sunrise.title: Teens Overnight Leaves
sunrise.desc: IT'S DAY ONE OF CAMP BABY!! We're leaving the main stage and going thrifting to build out our festival fits for the week. Bring some $ if you want to grab camp drip, or concert accessories for TeenLip Sync Battle, Bingo Night, and Silent Disco. Then comes our first true headliner: O V E R N I G H T. No VIP passes required. Just Tahoe air, s'mores, friends, and the first night of what will hopefully be a legendary week. We'll compete in an Iron Chef S'mores Contest, play card games, and make music videos. There may be a DMC or two, some spotlights, and a round of We're Not Really Strangers before sleeping under the stars.

sunrise.title: Church Service
sunrise.desc: The lovely Fallen Leaf Chapel is a short walk from Camp, just over the bridge.

sunrise.title: Ski Dock*
sunrise.desc: Sign up in the Foyer on Saturday night, or over at the Ski Dock for the rest of the week!

sunrise.title: Ospreys
sunrise.desc: As you step out onto the road, the lively sounds of cheering, laughter, and music echo for miles on end. The vibrant streaks of green, purple, and gold paint just about every inch of the historic city. The people, the smell, the feeling; all new and all very exciting. For a moment you begin to wonder, is this the best place I have ever been? The answer is short and clear: ABSO-FREAKING-LUTELY! Get ready to embark on an adventure of celebration, connection, and community that starts TODAY at the picnic tables. Will you be gold? Will you be green? Or maybe purple? Silver? Prepare for a morning of meeting your team and playing games that will showcase each group's strength, wit and persistence. "Laissez les bons temps rouler!" (Let the good times roll)!

noon.title: Open Climb
noon.desc: Come one, come all to the Sierra Camp rock wall! Whether you've never been climbing or you're Alex Honnold, Open Climb has a space for you. Join Paolo at the rock wall and ASCEND! All you need is yourself, some clothes for moving (including closed-toed shoes), and plenty of water. Parents and older kids are invited, along with younger kids under parental supervision. Bring just yourself or the whole family to see Sierra Camp from a whole new angle!

noon.title: Ospreys
noon.desc: The cool thing about celebrations is that they can happen anywhere, even on water. This afternoon the Mardi Graspreys will test their skills via stand up paddle board yoga. Please meet at 1pm at the picnic tables with swimsuit and sunscreen ready. Each team's balance and focus will be challenged with our PHENOMENAL yoga instructor Prisha. We can't wait to see the Mardi Graspreys for an afternoon of unwinding and community building that will set the stage for the journey yet to come.

noon.title: Outrigger Canoe*
noon.desc: Join our Outrigger Canoe enthusiasts, Heather & Tommy, for a unique opportunity to experience this special sport. Come be inspired by the rich history and current practices of outrigger canoeing! Meet on Baby Beach and don't forget to sign up at the office.

noon.title: Ski Dock*
noon.desc: Sign up at the Ski Dock.

noon.title: Beginner Sailing*
noon.desc: A little birdie told me you wanted to try out sailing! Cash will be at the boat dock to get you comfortable with the basics so you can start taking boats out on your own. You'll learn how to rig, use points of sail, and get some assisted lake practice. No sailing experience needed, just bring sunscreen, a swimsuit, and your biggest smile.

noon.title: Beginner Mountain Bike*
noon.desc: Want to get a workout in, but don't want to go for a run? How about a spin? Do you need a bike for your on-the-go lifestyle? Then the beginner ride is just the ride for you! Anyone can do it! Come join Ethan for the first bike ride of the week. Packing list: water, sunscreen, and your commitment to having fun! Meet at the bottom of the lodge at 1:45. See ya there!

noon.title: Art: Mosaic Magic*
noon.desc: Come join Andriana on the Old Lodge Deck for a hands-on mosaic session! We'll be working with colorful ceramic, and glass pieces to create your very own mosaic coaster. No experience needed, just bring clothes you don't mind getting a little dusty and your creative eye. Classical music, and calm, focused vibes guaranteed.

noon.title: Kids Groups (excluding Guppies)
noon.desc:
CRAWDADS: After a round of seafaring activities, ye shall prepare for the high seas by crafting your very own binoculars and telescopes. Then, we harden our sea legs and battle the waves with a special Yoga session. So, gear up with an outfit ye can move in. But beware! Rumour has it that the greatest pirate to sail these waters, the legendary Captain Sierra, hid their vast stash of treasure somewhere in the area, before they disappeared forever… Keep yer good eye peeled, Crawdads. We will end our voyage at 4pm in Angora.

MERGANSERS: After the investigation, our Mergansers will tie-dye their own bandanas, creating their own accessories that they can wear all week as they solve mysteries and explore the wonders of camp together. Finally, our tennis instructor Robert will teach them the ways of Federer and Nadal on the court to wrap up the day. Make sure you're ready for an adventure, ready to solve a mystery, and most importantly, ready to have fun! We will meet on the Angora deck. Please wear an outfit that is good for tennis!

CHICKAREES: After a midday break to study your spellbooks, the afternoon brings two more trials: tennis from 2–2:45pm, followed by rock climbing from 3–4pm. Show us what you're made of, Chickarees. The Tournament has begun. Come dressed for adventure, and in the afternoon, wear shoes fit for the court! Meet at the picnic tables!

MARMOTS: We will reconvene at the picnic tables and tactfully migrate towards the cliffs of insanity (rock wall) where we will strap on our safety gear and dodge pterodactyls in the sky as we clamber to the top! After this we will immortalize this prehistoric era by making nature-inspired UV cyanotype prints!

noon.title: Beginner Social Volleyball
noon.desc: Come meet Nat & Isa at the volleyball courts at 2:30pm! This activity is for players of all levels who just want to play some games for fun—less for competition. We will be playing 4vs4 and 6vs6. Bring your own beverages for some fun relaxation on the courts and in the sun! Music will be provided for your soul food.

noon.title: Bread Bake*
noon.desc: Knead something fun to fill your afternoon hours? It's time for bread baking! Come holla for Challah! Space is limited so be sure to sign up at the office, and meet Jordan & Yaara at the Dining Room Deck! Please arrive by 3:15pm at the latest if you want to participate.

noon.title: Language Hour
noon.desc: Eager to keep your brain sharp? Want to pick up some new phrases in other languages? Ben & Myron are excited to chat in languages such as Spanish, Mandarin Chinese, and French (wow!) and teach some new sayings. If you've never spoken these languages, no worries! Come stretch your brain and learn about another culture. Meet on the Old Lodge Deck!

noon.title: Book Talk: Arrow of God
noon.desc: In a world of increasing division and conflict, it's easy to be convinced our worldview and way of life are "correct." Chinua Achebe, often called the father of modern African literature, challenges many of our Western beliefs in Arrow of God (1964). The novel follows a chief priest in Igbo Nigeria as he navigates a society being reshaped by British colonizers and missionaries. Join Marcus at the Fountain Deck to grapple with Achebe's writing on culture, tradition, colonialism, and power—a perspective as relevant today as it was half a century ago.

noon.title: Story Hour
noon.desc: Meet Aylin, Isa, & Tamsin on the Cathedral Steps for some good old-fashioned storytelling fun. All ages welcome!

noon.title: Social Hike/Couples Photo
noon.desc: We call it a "hike," but it's more of a stroll – meet at the Fountain Deck at 5PM and venture to Lower Falls with us for some beautiful scenery and chats with fellow guests and staph! Get your photo taken by our Photo Instructor, Priyanka, with a beautiful backdrop behind you, too - see you there!

sunset.title: Family Portraits*
sunset.desc: The view is perfect, the kids are laughing, and there's no better time than now to capture this perfect summer getaway at Tahoe! Come down to the bottom of Chicken Hill to meet Priyanka and have your family portraits taken.

sunset.title: Multicultural Engagement Dinner
sunset.desc: In alignment with Stanford Sierra Camp's goal of uplifting inclusion and community, the Multicultural Engagement Dinner is a fun way for guests and staphers of all cultural backgrounds to participate in a safe and joyful space of cultural exchange over food and conversation. Within this space, we invite you to share life experiences, discuss your culture, impart knowledge, or share anything you find meaningful or helpful for the group as we navigate various stages and aspects of our lives. We ask that you engage respectfully. If interested, please join Melanie & Nat on the Ski Dock at 6:30 pm for an evening of bonding.

sunset.title: Boat Dock
sunset.desc: We're open! Come on down; the water's fine.

sunset.title: Guppy/Crawdad Puppet Show
sunset.desc: Looking for an epic, daring quest led by some world-renowned puppeteers? Come to the Tallac Room for an exciting show!

sunset.title: Chickaree/Merganser Night
sunset.desc: Feeling bummed because you didn't play Mafia enough today? Dying to learn how to make a friendship bracelet? Want to do the cha cha slide 50+ times? Calling all Mergansers and Chickarees--come to the Angora Room at 7:50pm, and we will make your dreams come TRUE! Can't wait to see you soon. :)

sunset.title: Faculty Speaker Lecture: AI Agent Simulation of Human Behavior w/ Michael Bernstein
sunset.desc: What if AI could simulate how people might behave? You could explore potential strategic decisions and understand how they might play out, test out your hunches in an instant, or see what conditions might need to be true for your ideas to succeed. In this presentation, Professor Bernstein dives into a frontier at the intersection of people and AI: AI agent simulations of human behavior. How do we build such agents, and how do we know whether they are accurate? Professor Bernstein will share an introduction to AI behavioral simulations, and use them to populate an interactive sandbox town of twenty five agents inspired by The Sims.

sunset.title: Marmots
sunset.desc: Get ready Marmots - now's the moment you're truly and officially initiated into our time travelling crew! You must complete a scavenger hunt to demonstrate your allegiance and time-travel abilities!

sunset.title: Ospreys
sunset.desc: Although they would never really admit it to themselves, every group really wants to be a Mardi Grasprey. However, few know that in order to be one, you must go through a super secret inaugural event and word on the street is that it is OSP-OME. After, we will be venturing to a GLOW IN THE DARK DODGEBALL ARENA. Here, teams will gear up in neon and glow galore and compete for the ultimate prize of being dodgeball champion. This is an evening you do NOT want to miss. Meet at the picnic tables at 8 pm sharp.

sunset.title: Fountain Pretzel Night
sunset.desc: Fountain Pretzel Night! Come get a delicious soft pretzel available throughout tonight's Fountain hours!

sunset.title: Astrocruise*
sunset.desc: What better way to spend a night at Camp than under an endless sea of stars and constellations? Join Kyle & Melanie on a nighttime cruise around the lake to ponder deep philosophical conversations, listen to tragedies of Orion and Scorpius, or simply revel in the beauty of our existence in this universe. Embark on this journey by signing up at the office and bundling up in some layers before meeting out at the Boat Dock to get astrocruisin'!

sunrise.title: Rowing*
sunrise.desc: Join Cash & Priyanka in gliding across the glassy waters at the most beautiful time of the day! Expert rower or never rowed before? No worries! Sign up for a boat outside the office beforehand and we can help you get going! See ya at the rowing dock!

sunrise.title: Ski Dock*
sunrise.desc: Sign up at Ski Dock.

sunrise.title: Long Hike*
sunrise.desc: Time to seize the day! What better way to do that than by going on a nice little trot in the woods? And by trot, we will not be running (thank goodness), but we will be on the move for most of the day today. Yippee, time to carpe diem in the good ol mountains. Make sure to bring LOTS (3+ liters) of water, hiking shoes, sunscreen, lunch + snacks, and your best joke for Jack to hear. Fountain Deck at 8:15am!

sunrise.title: Guppies
sunrise.desc: The jungle calls, and today we answer! At 9:15am, we will lace up our boots and head out on a grand jungle expedition (a hike to the Fallen Leaf Lake fire station). Before the trek, we'll warm up with a Jungle Freeze Dance to get those wild instincts going. On the hike, we will meet some firefighters and might just get to go in the fire truck, if we are lucky. After our hike, explorers will wind down with jungle storytime, drawing what we discovered on the trail, and crafting our very own animal masks from paper plates. Pack a full lunch, sunscreen, and a water bottle, and make sure those shoes are closed-toed and ready for the trail!

sunrise.title: Advanced Tennis Clinic: Boot Camp*
sunrise.desc: USTA 3.0-4.5 recommended. Sierra Camp's most intense clinic. While here, Robert will take you through a high energy and physically challenging drill session, with fast-paced games, king-of-the-hill style points, and the opportunity to witness a post-prime Robert going all out. Come if you're looking to break a big sweat, or if you're scouting for a challenge!

sunrise.title: Kids Groups
sunrise.desc:
CRAWDADS: Ahoy mateys! Today we will set sail on a grand hiking adventure. We will begin our journey in the Angora Room at 9:30 am, where we will gather supplies and prepare to embark on our voyage. Our first stop will be at the land where fire goes to be vanquished—the fire station! From there, we will traverse the depths of Glen Alpine, keeping a careful eye out for rival armadas and elusive mermaids, until we reach the land of hidden treasure: Lily Lake. Here, we will collect our booty and celebrate with a scrumptious lunch, before returning to the maiden land for a crash course taught by our resident Kids Naturalist and fun end-of-day quests. Adventure awaits! For our grand expedition, we ask for the following to prevent mutiny: a packed lunch to eat at Lily Lake, water to stay hydrated, and an otherwise (mostly) empty backpack to keep our weight light!

MERGANSERS: Mystery, intrigue, stolen flags, broken hearts and deceit. This case stinks to high heaven, and the Merganser Detectives need some fresh air. Gather at the Angora Deck at 9:30am so we can go to Baby Beach for some R&R! There's nothing like building sandcastles and splashing around to help us gather our thoughts… and some fresh clues while we're at it. With any luck we'll track down our star witness, the Kids Naturalist, Sydney, who saw the whole thing go down. After some time at the beach, each detective will get the chance to make their mark on this case when we play Paint Your Counselor! Bring an inquisitive eye and suspicious energy to this awesome Beach Day (and also a swimsuit, towel, sunscreen, and a packed lunch).

CHICKAREES: Hear ye, young witches and wizards! The Triwizard Tournament is about to begin. Meet at the Picnic Tables at 9:30am where you'll prepare for a day of magical competition at the beach. Through enchanted beach challenges, teamwork games, and wizarding contests, houses will earn points as they compete for glory. Bring your house pride, adventurous spirit, and readiness for a little bit of magic as we begin our journey toward becoming legendary Chickaree champions! Please also bring a packed lunch, a swimsuit, and a towel!

MARMOTS: The time machine has been set for the year 25,000. It's time to experience the future. Beep beep. Boop boop. WHOOOSH. However, as we step outside, something's wrong. There are no flying cars or futuristic skyscrapers. Instead, there are just plants and rocks? Silly us! We've travelled to 25,000 BCE! A man wearing a majestic tiger pelt emerges from a nearby cave. A caveman??? He seems unfazed by the time machine. He walks up to us and says: "Welcome. Me Grog. Today Hike Day. Go Picnic Table at nine thirty. Bring water. Bring Sunscreen. Unk." We're speechless. The English language isn't supposed to be invented for another 26,000 years! Before we can ask him any questions, he continues: "Also pack a lunch and bring some cash if you'd like to buy a lemonade at the top of Angora. It's really expensive for what it is, but they kind of have a monopoly on the cold drinks up there so they can really charge whatever they want. This economy, amirite... Unk." Grog abruptly turns around and heads back to his cave. Well, we have some time to kill before the machine recharges. Let's go hike! I heard there's a lake up there so bring a swimsuit if you wanna swim!

sunrise.title: Pickleball Play
sunrise.desc: Ticklin' for a picklin'? Pickleball that is. This sport is all the rage and it's found its way into the heart of Camp. Join Harrshwin at the basketball court, or, as we've rebranded it, The Pickleball™ Court for a few friendly matches.

sunrise.title: Craft: Shrinky Dink*
sunrise.desc: Ever loved something so much you wanted to shrink it and carry it about in your pocket? We'll be drawing on special plastic that shrinks when heated to create keychains and jewelry. Meet Nia at the Old Lodge Deck!

sunrise.title: Faculty Speaker Debrief/Follow-Up
sunrise.desc: Eager to parse through last night's lecture? Meet Chiara and our lovely faculty speaker on the Fountain Deck for more discussion and excellent questions!

sunrise.title: Cardio Yoga
sunrise.desc: Get powered up as the morning birds begin to sing by joining us for pilates with Prisha. Build endurance and strength while having fun! Bring water and your wonderful self. Meet us at the CrawDeck (used to be the Snooper Chalet!)

sunrise.title: Ospreys: Beach Day
sunrise.desc: Encapsulating the beauty of Osprey Beach Day can only be accomplished through poetry. Enjoy this perfectly curated masterpiece: Up at Kiva Beach, where the blue waves play, The Ospreys are soaring into Beach Day! With Tahoe sun and sand beneath our feet, We'll dive into games where fun and friends meet. Volleyballs flying, frisbees in the air, Soccer on the shoreline, spikeball with flair. We'll run, we'll laugh, we'll splash, we'll cheer, Making the most of summer right here. For those who want a slower pace, There's time to relax in a sunny place. Nails to paint, henna to try, Or just tanning beneath the mountain sky. Then when the day begins to slow, There's one last stop before we go: Ice cream scoops to end the scene, The sweetest Beach Day there's ever been! Meet at the flagpole with supplies in hand. Survival Kit: Sunscreen, Water, Swimsuit, Towel, Packed lunch, Ice cream funds

noon.title: Ski Dock*
noon.desc: Sign up at the Ski Dock.

noon.title: Staph to Guest
noon.desc: Interested in listening to diverse life stories from all walks of life? Interested in connecting with fellow guests and staphers on a deeper level? Ready to engage in facilitated dialogue about identity and the ways in which we become who we are today? Staph-to-Guest is here for you! A beloved Camp tradition, come join Yaara & Jason in the Old Lodge for an hour of personal storytelling, fruitful conversation, and bidirectional vulnerability. We can't wait to share our stories and hear yours!

noon.title: Beginner Sailing*
noon.desc: A little birdie told me you wanted to try out sailing! Cash will be at the boat dock to get you comfortable with the basics so you can start taking boats out on your own. You'll learn how to rig, use points of sail, and get some assisted lake practice. No sailing experience needed, just bring sunscreen, a swimsuit, and your biggest smile.

noon.title: Craft: Bisqueware*
noon.desc: Have you been eyeing a specific piece of bisqueware at the fountain? This is your chance to pick it up from the fountain and come glaze it with Jan. Join us at the tables by the volleyball court to customize your very own piece of bisqueware. Sign-up for crafts!

noon.title: Naturalist Kayak*
noon.desc: Come join Isaiah at the boat dock at 2:30pm to set off and see the Fallen Leaf basin from the water. You'll hear about birds, look for plants, and hear about the history of the land. Can't wait to see you there!

noon.title: Open Climb
noon.desc: Come one, come all to the Sierra Camp rock wall! Whether you've never been climbing or you're Alex Honnold, Open Climb has a space for you. Join Paolo at the rock wall and ASCEND! All you need is yourself, some clothes for moving (including closed-toed shoes), and plenty of water. Parents and older kids are invited, along with younger kids under parental supervision. Bring just yourself or the whole family to see Sierra Camp from a whole new angle!

noon.title: Teen Sail
noon.desc: Be there or be square. This will be fun. Meet Cash at the boat dock at 3pm for the ride of your life!

noon.title: Multicultural Engagement Social
noon.desc: In alignment with Stanford Sierra Camp's goal of uplifting community and inclusion, the Multicultural Engagement Social is for guests and staphers of all cultural backgrounds to create meaningful connections and relationships! Within this space, we invite you to share life experiences, discuss your culture, impart knowledge, or share anything you find meaningful or helpful for the group as we navigate various stages and aspects of our lives. We will be centering the voices and stories of our multicultural participants and ask that if you are not someone who holds a multicultural experience to come learn and practice engaging respectfully. If interested, come meet with Aiden & Isa on the Old Lodge Deck at 4PM for a good time with amazing company! ENABs and charcuterie provided.

noon.title: Social Volleyball
noon.desc: Hit the sand courts for some easygoing bump, setting, and spiking. All ages welcome. Matt is excited for some friendly 4v4 competition this afternoon – see you there!

noon.title: Story Hour
noon.desc: This afternoon, buckle up to leave SSC and blast off into the world of stories! The destination is anywhere and everywhere --- we can go to far off planets, to the homes of rabbits, to a battle between witches and warlocks...the possibilities are endless! Just show up at the Cathedral Steps at 5pm to meet Prisha & Murph for this storytelling journey!

noon.title: Music Trivia Boatster Cruise*
noon.desc: Music and memories. Need we say more? Join Josh & Ben on the Boat Dock for a happy hour on the water, now with a twist – music trivia! A time for relaxation, good company, and toe-tapping tunes. Grab a blanket from the dock, put on your sea legs, and test your musical know-how as we cruise. An hour you won't forget!

sunrise.title: Forest Yoga: Back to the Trees*
Arts: Oil Painting - Fruit & Still Life*

sunset.title: Trivia Night: Music of the Decades

sunrise.title: SUP Yoga: Out of Your Element*
Reflection Yoga: A Date with Yourself

noon.title: Psychology Discussion: Topics in Social Psychology

groups: Assorted Deli Selections with Fresh Toppings and Whole Grain Breads
Red Beans w/ Celery, Bell Pepper, Garlic, and Onion

Assorted Juices and Local Roast Coffees
